_readme: Troy's EYL University Option Classes — source of truth for all picks. Add a new class entry whenever Troy covers new names. troy-monitor.py reads this file and auto-injects new tickers into the dashboard.

classes.label: May '26 Options Masterclass
classes.notes: Semiconductor + AI infrastructure deep dive. PDF available.

classes.label: Apr '26 Class
classes.notes: Memory super-cycle deep dive — DRAM ETF as SK Hynix/Samsung proxy. Troy placed LIVE DRAM order during class: Jan '27 $33C, 20 contracts, $9.70 limit, stop trigger at $30 stock price. DRAM ran to $35.12 before filling. MU referenced as prior community board pick (up 60% since Apr 1 call). No PDF — reconstructed from transcript.

classes.label: Mar '26 Class
classes.notes: Micron earnings review (beat but stock sold off). Photonics deep-dive — GLW (Corning) + LITE (Lumentum) earnings blowout. Fiber optics as AI data center infrastructure play. No new contracts given; reinforcement of existing thesis. No PDF.

classes.label: Feb '26 Class (feat. Illio)
classes.notes: EDA (Electronic Design Automation) thesis: NVDA $2B investment in Synopsys signals AI chip design software boom. SNPS 52-week high $651, pulled back 30% to $442. Troy bought Jun '26 $440C at ~$30/contract ($3,000 ea). CDNS already in portfolio up 13%. Illio options income demo in second half. No PDF.

classes.label: Dec '25 Class
classes.notes: Energy sector deep-dive. CEG (Constellation Energy) — nuclear power + AI data center thesis. Morning star pattern off support; prior bull run to $413. VST (Vistra) — 20-year nuclear power purchase agreement with hyperscaler (Comanche Park plant). AMD recap: reviewed the journey from the April 2025 class entry through the bounce. No specific entry prices given. No PDF.

classes.label: Nov '25 Class
classes.notes: Portfolio returns review: NVDA $115C +92%, NVDA $120C +134%, TSM +168%, AMD +176%, LLY +240%, CLS +240%, CRWD +150%, AVGO +276%, April SMH call +281%. WDC (SanDisk spinoff, ticker may be SNDK) introduced: AI data center SSD/flash memory play for LLM training model storage. GLW ongoing thesis. No PDF.

classes.label: Oct '25 Class
classes.notes: Portfolio rotation class — rotating Dec 2025 expiries into Jan 2026/2027 LEAPS. Corning (GLW) introduced: Apple iPhone 17 glass + AI data center fiber optics thesis, up 83% over prior 12 months. Broadcom-Corning partnership highlight. No specific contract prices given for GLW. No PDF.

classes.label: Jul '25 Class
classes.notes: TSM and AMD continuing thesis. CLS (Celestica Inc.) briefly mentioned as a new position ('call number three') — AI server and data center hardware play. No specific contracts given. No PDF.

classes.label: Jun '25 Class — Hedging & Puts
classes.notes: Educational class focused on downside protection: put options, short ETFs, and using Barchart tools for screening. Strategy class — no new buy picks introduced. Context: tariff uncertainty from April 2 Liberation Day still affecting markets. No PDF.

classes.label: Apr '25 Class — Tariff Pullback
classes.notes: Tariff 'Liberation Day' (April 2) reaction class. SMH (VanEck Semiconductor ETF) used as live teaching example — options fell from $6,107/contract peak on March 28 to ~$3,000 post-tariff. Taught deep-in-the-money strategy (Nancy Pelosi approach: buy $120C when stock at $193). AMD heavily discussed as buy on the tariff-driven pullback. No specific entry alert given. No PDF.

classes.label: Mar '25 Class
classes.notes: TSM and AMD continuing thesis reinforcement. No new picks or contracts introduced. Market context: early 2025 consolidation after strong 2024. No PDF.

classes.label: Jan '25 Class
classes.notes: Deep option mechanics class using TSM as the live example. Troy revealed his TSM $105C LEAP position — sold 14 contracts on Jan 3, kept 1 for the earnings catalyst (Jan 15 earnings, Jan 17 expiry). VST at $198 — noted Nancy Pelosi bought Dec '26 calls (deep-in-the-money LEAP). VRT — moved from $118 to $153. Reinforced LEAP strategy using in-the-money calls. No PDF.

classes.label: Dec '24 — Top Stocks for 2025
classes.notes: Annual top stocks class — Troy named 10 picks for 2025. TSM as stock of the year ('slap calls the day it breaks above $203/$340'). AMZN as co-stock of the year ('one A, one B' with TSM). AMD heavily discussed. TSM Arizona fab update — expected production late 2026. No specific contract prices given. No PDF.

classes.label: Nov '24 Class
classes.notes: Portfolio review and thesis reinforcement. TSM, VRT, AMD, VST all discussed as active positions. Data center cooling (VRT) and nuclear/power (VST) emerging as new AI infrastructure plays alongside semiconductors. No specific contracts given. No PDF.

classes.label: Oct '24 Class
classes.notes: AMD buy-the-pullback thesis: 52-week high was $229, pulled back to $154.74 at class time. Troy said 'I really like this AMD call' ahead of upcoming earnings. TSM ongoing. Microsoft/MSFT discussed as stock + option combo strategy (own shares to justify option position). No specific contracts given. No PDF.

classes.label: Sep '24 Class
classes.notes: Early portfolio-building class. TSM, AMD, and SMH ETF as semiconductor ecosystem plays. Portfolio strategy education — LEAPS, time value, theta decay. Referenced introducing WDC (Western Digital + Seagate) as memory/storage plays in this era. No specific contracts given. No PDF.

classes.label: Jul '24 Class (feat. Caleb Silver)
classes.notes: Guest class with Caleb Silver (Investopedia). AMD and TSM as core semiconductor thesis. SMCI (Super Micro Computer) briefly discussed — AI server hardware. Educational format covering option greeks and LEAP strategy basics. No specific contracts given. No PDF.

classes.label: Jun '24 Class Vol.4
classes.notes: Vol.4 of the master class series. CDNS (Cadence Design Systems) appears — early EDA thesis before the NVDA/Synopsys announcement. AMD, AVGO (Broadcom), and CRM (Salesforce) discussed as AI software/hardware plays. No specific contracts given. No PDF.

classes.label: May '24 Class Vol.3 — Options Fundamentals
classes.notes: Foundational options education class (PowerPoint format). Covered: what options are, calls vs puts, contracts (1 contract = 100 shares), strike price, expiration dates, bid/ask spread, reading an option chain, contract multiplier, and Troy's core buy strategy (find calls 40% below alert price — 'finding undervalued contracts'). Key quote: 'If META stock grows 5%, the option can grow 50% — the stock grew $15, the option grew $350.' Next targets given as stock price alerts: VISA $267-270, AAPL 'soon', NFLX $535-540, TMO $552-558. Source: uploaded PowerPoint eyl-options-may-9th.pptx.

stocks.name: Apple Inc.
stocks._note: Troy said 'soon' — no specific price given

stocks.name: Netflix Inc.
stocks._note: Stock alert $535-540 given in class (2024 prices)

stocks.name: Thermo Fisher Scientific
stocks._note: Stock alert $552-558 given in class (2024 prices)

classes.label: Mar '24 Class Vol.1 — First Options Class
classes.notes: PENDING — Vol.1, the original EYL options masterclass. No subtitle file available. User will provide screenshots for content extraction.

watchlist_only._note: Stocks Troy has discussed / in his book but no specific class entry yet. Add alert price when Troy gives one.

stocks.reason: Nuclear power + AI data centers — Dec '25 class

stocks.reason: Nuclear PPA + AI power demand — Dec '25 class

how_to_add_new_class.step1: Copy the class template below into the 'classes' array above (newest first)
how_to_add_new_class.step2: Fill in date, label, notes, and the stocks Troy covered with their contracts
how_to_add_new_class.step3: Set alert to Troy's entry price if he gave one, null if not
how_to_add_new_class.step4: Commit and push — troy-monitor.py will auto-add any new tickers to the dashboard

template.label: Month 'YY Class
template.notes: Brief description of what was covered
